operations.summary: Update an existing generic device in the Topology model
operations.notes:
Modify an existing generic device by uploading a new configuration file and/or specifying a new name.
If name field is specified, the generic device will be renamed.
configuration_file is optional, if provided it must include a valid generic device configuration.
The specified path points to a file in the client machine.
If a generic device name is changed and a new configuration file is not provided, topology will not be synchronized (update_topology flag is ignored).
Default value for update_topology = true.
To save time when there are several consecutive api calls: For the last call, set update_topology = true (for all other calls, update_topology = false).
For more information on input file formats, refer to Knowledge Center topic 'Adding and Updating a Generic Device'.

responseMessages.message: The generic device was updated successfully.
A new name or a new configuration file must be indicated in order to update generic device with id {}
Failed to update generic device with id {}
A 'Generic Device' with the specified ID was not found

operations.summary: Delete generic device from Topology model
operations.notes:
Delete a generic device from the Topology model.
The ID must be for a valid generic device listed in the Topology Model.
If update update_topology = true, then the Topology model will be updated immediately after the device is deleted.
For maximum efficiency, if you are deleting multiple generic devices in consecutive API calls, you can set update_topology = false on all API calls except for the last one. On the last call, set update_toplogy = true.
Default value for update_topology = true.

responseMessages.message: Failed to delete generic device with id {}
A 'Generic Device' with the specified ID was not found

operations.summary: Export configuration file of specific generic device
operations.notes:
Export configuration file of specific generic device.
The ID must be for a valid generic device listed in the Topology Model.

responseMessages.message: Generic device configuration file exported successfully.
Failed to export configuration file of generic device with id {}
User is not permitted to access the specified domain.
Not found configuration file of generic device with id {}.
A generic device with the specified ID was not found

operations.summary: Get generic devices that are configured in ST
operations.notes:
Returns a list of the generic devices included in all the domains that the user has permission to.
Use context and/or name optional parameters to get more specific results.
To get context optional parameter, you can call /domains/ API and use the id included within the returned domain DTO.

operations.summary: Add generic device to Topology model
operations.notes:
Upload a configuration file that will be used to add a generic device to the Topology model.
The context field is the domain ID to which the generic device is added: If not provided, the device is added to the global domain.
configuration_file must include a valid generic device configuration.
The specified path must point to a file on the computer being sue to make the REST API call.
If update update_topology = true, then the Topology model will be updated immediately after the device is added.
For maximum efficiency, if you are adding multiple generic devices in consecutive API calls, you can set update_topology = false on all API calls except for the last one. On the last call, set update_toplogy = true.Default value for update_topology = true.
For more information on input file formats, refer to Knowledge Center topic 'Adding and Updating a Generic Device'.

responseMessages.message: The generic device was created successfully
User is not permitted to access the specified domain

operations.summary: Get path pdf report for specified traffic
operations.notes:
Returns an pdf report containing one or more paths for the specified traffic.
src and dst parameters may contain multiple, comma separated values as following:
1. Host / subnet ip address, either ipv4 or ipv6 (for example: "1.2.3.4", "5.6.7.0:24", "8.9.0.0:255.255.0.0", "2222::1:128")
2. Device network object name (for example: "netobj:422:net_22.22.22.0" refers to network object named "net_22.22.22.0" related to device with id 422)
3. LDAP Group (for example: "LDAP:cn=grp1:cn=users:dc=tcse:dc=net")
4. NSX security group name (for example: "SG:189:securitygroup-11" refers to a security group named "securitygroup-11", related to NSX root device with id 189)
5. ACI EPG / External EPG / ACI ESG (for example: "EPG:505:TN_ATU_MGMT:ApplicationProfile1:EPG_02", "EXTEPG:505:TN_ATU_MGMT:L3OUT-VRF1:ext_epg1", "ESG:505:TN_ATU_MGMT:ApplicationProfile1:ESG_1")
6. A specific endpoint within ACI External EPG (for example: 30.40.50.60@EXTEPG:505:TN_ATU_MGMT:L3OUT-VRF1:ext_epg1)
7. URL category (destination only, Panorama devices only, for example: UC:my_sport_urls)
8. DNS (for example: DNS:tufin.com)

Service parameter can be "any" or any comma separated combination of the following values:
1. Protocol and port (for example, "tcp:80")
2. A service name (for example: "ftp", "syslog")
3. An application with its default services, case sensitive (for example, "Skype", "Facebook")
4. An application with overridden services, case sensitive (for example, "Skype@tcp:44", "Facebook@(tcp:44,tcp:55)")
5. IP protocol number (for example, "58" for IPv6-ICMP)
If sourceDomainIdTag is provided, the path starting points will be restricted to the specified domain ID. sourceDomainIdTag is valid only if overlapping IP mode is enabled.
If context is provided, the path will be calculated for the specified domain context, otherwise the default domain context is used. Context is valid only if MSSP mode is enabled.
To get the context, call the /domains/ API and use the ID included within the returned domain DTO.
displayIncompletePaths parameter default value is false, if this parameter is set to true -
all nodes on the path will be displayed, even if the node cannot reach the destination, or if the source and destination are the same.
displayBlockedStatus parameter default value is false, if this parameter is set to true -
blocked traffic icons will be displayed next to devices blocking the traffic.
simulateNat parameter default value is true if this parameter is set to false - NAT simulation will not be used when calculating the path.
lastInstall parameter default value is true, which means simulation would use last installed policies. If this parameter is set to false - simulation would use the last saved policies.
pathIds parameter default value is empty, which means that all paths would be exported to the report.
This API cannot be executed more than 10 times in parallel.

6. A specific endpoint within ACI External EPG (for example: 30.40.50.60@EXTEPG:505:TN_ATU_MGMT:L3OUT-VRF1:ext_epg1)
7. URL category (destination only, Panorama devices only, for example: UC:my_sport_urls)
8. DNS (for example: DNS:tufin.com)

Service parameter can be "any" or any comma separated combination of the following values:
1. Protocol and port (for example: "tcp:80")
2. A service name (for example: "ftp", "syslog")
3. IP protocol number (for example: "58" for IPv6-ICMP)
If sourceDomainIdTag is provided, the path starting points will be restricted to the specified domain ID. sourceDomainIdTag is valid only if overlapping IP mode is enabled.
If context is provided, the path will be calculated for the specified domain context, otherwise the default domain context is used. Context is valid only if MSSP mode is enabled.
To get the context, call the /domains/ API and use the ID included within the returned domain DTO.
includeIncompletePaths parameter default value is false, if this parameter is set to true -
all nodes on the path will be included, even if the node cannot reach the destination, or if the source and destination are the same.
simulateNat parameter default value is true if this parameter is set to false - NAT simulation will not be used when calculating the path.
lastInstall parameter default value is true, which means simulation would use last installed policies. If this parameter is set to false - simulation would use the last saved policies.
This API cannot be executed more than 10 times in parallel.

operations.summary: Create or update generic Route Maps
operations.notes:
This API allows creating or updating one or more generic route maps for a specific network device.

Each route map must contain:
- A human-readable `name` (unique identifier for the route map)
- A list of `interfaces` where the route map is applied
- A list of `entries`, where each entry defines policy-based routing behavior

Entry Parameters:

- seq_number: Unique sequence number that defines evaluation order within the route map
- matcher_policy_name: Name of the generic policy that defines traffic matching criteria
- Must reference an existing generic policy created via the generic policy APIs
- See: POST /topology/generic/policy (create policy)
- See: GET /topology/generic/policy (retrieve policies)
- The referenced policy contains rules that define source, destination, service, and action criteria
- entry_action: Action to take when traffic matches the policy ("Accept" or "Deny")
- actions: Array of routing actions to apply when entry matches and is accepted

Routing Action Parameters:

- nexthop: IP address of the next hop router (IPv4/IPv6), e.g. "10.0.0.1""
- Use null if not setting a specific next hop
- interface: Name of the egress interface, e.g. "GigabitEthernet0/1", "FastEthernet0/0"
- Use null if not setting a specific interface
- vrf: Virtual Routing and Forwarding (VRF) instance name
- Use null for default/global routing table
- global_vrf: Boolean flag indicating if the VRF is global (typically false)

General Notes:
- The `route_maps` array supports defining multiple independent route maps for the device
- Route map entries are evaluated sequentially based on `seq_number`
- At least one routing action (nexthop or interface) should be specified per entry
- VRF and interface/nexthop combinations enable advanced policy-based routing scenarios
- The API performs create or update operations based on existing route map configuration
- All string values are case-sensitive

Policy Integration:
- Route maps work in conjunction with generic policies for policy-based routing
- Generic policies define WHAT traffic to match (source, destination, service criteria)
- Route maps define WHERE to route that traffic (nexthop, interface, VRF)
- Workflow: 1) Create generic policies using POST /topology/generic/policy
2) Reference those policy names in route map matcher_policy_name fields
3) Define routing actions for matched traffic in the route map entries
Get generic Route Maps of a device.
Delete all generic Route Maps from a device.
Join/Split Subnet.

Returns a list of the topology subnets for all the domains for which the user has permission to access.
This API requires either 'Super admin' or 'Multi-Domain admin' privileges.
For users with 'Super admin' permission, the context parameter is optional. If a context is not specified, the Global context will be used.
For users with 'Multi-Domain' privilege, the context parameter is required.
To get the context parameter, call the /domains/ API and use the id included in the returned domain DTO.
Use the optional name parameter to restrict the results to topology subnet names that match the search criteria provided, as follows:
1. If you provide only a mask, then an exact match search is done on the mask portion of the name
e.g. if name=/2, the results will include 1.2.3.4/2, but not 1.2.3.4/22
2. If you provide only a partial or complete IP address, then a contains search is done in the IPv4 block portion of the name
e.g. name=32, the results will include 192.168.205.32/24 and 55.192.32.22/16, but will not include 55.168.205.5/32
3. If you provide both an IPv4 address (either partial or complete) and a mask, then an exact match search is done
e.g. if name=23.4/2, then the results will include 192.168.23.4/2, but will not include 192.168.23.4/23 or 23.4.192.168/2

Use the optional ip parameter to restrict the results to topology subnets that contain this ip, see example below.

User can use ip or name parameter, not both.
The default pagination is start=0 and count=50.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: The starting element offset to include in the returned results

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Number of elements to include in the results

responseMessages.message: Only users with 'Super admin' permissions can access Global domain map
You do not have access permission to domain id: domainId
Using name and ip parameters is not allowed
Using Ipv6 in ip parameter is not supported

operations.notes:
This API allows creating one or more generic security policies with associated traffic rules.

  Each one must contain:
   A human-readable `name`
   A list of `rules`, where each rule defines traffic filtering behavior

   Rule Parameters:

   - Source / Destination: Accepts multiple values in the following formats:
    - IP address (IPv4/IPv6), e.g. "192.168.1.1", "2001:db8::1"
    - Subnet (CIDR), e.g. "192.168.1.0/24", "2001:db8::/64"
    - IP range, e.g. "10.0.0.1-10.0.0.50"
    - "Any" (case-insensitive)

   - Service: Supports combinations like:
    - "tcp", "udp", "icmp"
    - "tcp:80", "udp:53", "icmp:1"
    - Port ranges, e.g. "udp:100-200"
    - Protocol numbers, e.g. "1:12" or "17:100-102"
    - "Any" (case-insensitive)

   - Action: "ACCEPT" or "DENY"

   - Rule_number: Unique per policy; defines evaluation order

  General Notes:
   The `generic_policies` array supports defining multiple independent policies
   Each policy must include one or more rules
   Rules are evaluated sequentially based on `rule_number`
   All string values are case-sensitive unless noted otherwise

This API allows updating one or more generic security policies with associated traffic rules.

  Each one must contain:
   A unique `id` (UUID format)
   A human-readable `name`
   A list of `rules`, where each rule defines traffic filtering behavior

   Rule Parameters:

   - Source / Destination: Accepts multiple values in the following formats:
    - IP address (IPv4/IPv6), e.g. "192.168.1.1", "2001:db8::1"
    - Subnet (CIDR), e.g. "192.168.1.0/24", "2001:db8::/64"
    - IP range, e.g. "10.0.0.1-10.0.0.50"
    - "Any" (case-insensitive)

   - Service: Supports combinations like:
    - "tcp", "udp", "icmp"
    - "tcp:80", "udp:53", "icmp:1"
    - Port ranges, e.g. "udp:100-200"
    - Protocol numbers, e.g. "1:12" or "17:100-102"
    - "Any" (case-insensitive)

   - Action: "ACCEPT" or "DENY"

   - Rule_number: Unique per policy; defines evaluation order

  General Notes:
   The `generic_policies` array supports defining multiple independent policies
   Each policy must include one or more rules
   Rules are evaluated sequentially based on `rule_number`
   All string values are case-sensitive unless noted otherwise

operations.summary: Get topology routing tables for a given device
operations.notes:
The returned routing tables are taken from the topology data and will include only devices that are topology enabled.
When adding a device, the user can can choose whether to use dynamic or static topology for the device,
this choice will determine which kind of data is stored in the topology graph and returned by this API.

The parameter mgmtId should be a cluster or a gateway, not a management server.
You may provide a generic device Id (see generic_devices API under 'Monitored Devices' category) -
in this case make sure you set 'is_generic' parameter to 'true'

parameters.name: mgmtId
parameters.description: Management Id or Generic Device Id to query

parameters.name: is_generic
parameters.description: Should be set to 'true' if the provided mgmtId is related to generic device

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Starting page for query allowable type is numeric

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Number of pages for query from starting page, allowable type is numeric

operations.summary: Get topology network interfaces by device
operations.notes:
The returned interfaces are taken from the topology data and will include only devices that are topology enabled.
When adding a device, the user can can choose whether to use dynamic or static topology for the device,
this choice will determine which kind of data is stored in the topology graph and returned by this API.

The parameter mgmtId should be a cluster or a gateway, not a management server.
You may provide a generic device Id (see generic_devices API under 'Monitored Devices' category) -
in this case make sure you set 'is_generic' parameter to 'true'

parameters.name: mgmtId
parameters.description: Management Id or Generic Device Id to query

parameters.name: is_generic
parameters.description: Should be set to 'true' if the provided mgmtId is related to generic device

operations.notes:
get topology setting details.
1. Synchronize timing details. [These details requires 'Super admin' privileges].
2. Overlapping IP field:
     overlappingIp field - Boolean value indicating whether overlapping IP mode is enabled.
3. Export collapsed map view:
     exportImageCollapsed field - When enabled, device groups and hidden items are excluded from the exported image.

responseMessages.message: get topology setting details was invoked successfully.

operations.notes:
set topology setting details.
1. Synchronize timing fields [These details requires 'Super admin' privileges]:
     synchType field - There are only two valid values: WEEKLY, DAILY.
     hour field - Valid values: 0 - 23.
     minutes field - Valid values: 0 - 59.
     dayOfWeek field - Valid values: 0 - 7.
2. Overlapping IP field:
     overlappingIp field - Boolean value indicating whether overlapping IP mode is enabled.
3. Export collapsed map view:
     exportImageCollapsed field - When enabled, device groups and hidden items are excluded from the exported image.

responseMessages.message: set topology setting details was invoked successfully.
Service unavailable, Graph builder is currently running

operations.notes:
Invoke of the Topology Model Synchronization.
full_sync=false - Builds the topology model from the information stored in the Tufin database.
full_sync=true - Retrieves the current data from devices and builds the topology model.
Default value for full_sync = false.

responseMessages.message: The topology synchronization was invoked successfully.
Service unavailable, Graph builder is currently running

operations.notes: Returns the progress status of the Topology Model Synchronization.

responseMessages.message: The topology synchronization was invoked successfully.

operations.summary: Get Device topology generic VPN connections
operations.notes:
Returns a list of the generic vpn connections in the Topology map, for all the domains for which the user has permission to access.
This API requires either 'Super admin' or 'Multi-Domain admin' privileges.
For users with 'Super admin' permission, the context parameter is optional. If a context is not specified, the Global context will be used.
For users with 'Multi-Domain' privilege, the context parameter is required.
To get the context parameter, call the /domains/ API and use the id included in the returned domain DTO.
Use the optional name parameter to restrict the results to topology generic vpn connections that contain the specified string.
The default pagination is start=0 and count=50.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: The starting element offset to include in the returned results

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Number of elements to include in the results

responseMessages.message: Only users with 'Super admin' permissions can access Global domain map
You do not have access permission to domain id: domainId
Wrong type provided - allowable values are: joined or non-joined

Returns the topology cloud data for the specified id.
This API requires 'Super admin' or 'Multi-Domain admin' privileges.
Multi-Domain user must have access permission to the domain where the cloud resides.

responseMessages.message: You do not have access permission to the domain for cloud with id ID
No cloud was found for the provided ID

operations.notes:
Modify the name of a topology cloud, and add/remove members of a joined cloud.
This API requires either 'Super admin' or 'Multi-Domain admin' privileges.
Multi-Domain user must have access permission to the domain context of the joined cloud, and any members specified in the body.
To change the name of a topology cloud, enter a value in the name field of the body.
To add/remove members of a joined cloud, specify the action and specify the clouds in the members list of the body.
The action is used only if the body contains a members list. If a members list is provided but no actions is specified, then the default action is 'add'.
When adding cloud members, clouds included in the members list of the body must not be joined clouds or members of another joined cloud.
When removing cloud members, if only zero or one member remains in the joined cloud, it will be deleted from the Interactive Map.

responseMessages.message: You do not have access permission to the domain for cloud with id ID
Cloud with id ID was not found in the provided MSSP domain
Cloud with id ID is a not joined cloud
Cloud id: cloudId does not belong to domain: Context
Provided clouds don't belongs to the same domain
Cloud with id ID is a joined cloud
Cloud with id ID is a member of a joined cloud
Member cloud with id ID cannot be removed - it is not a member of a joined cloud

operations.notes:
Returns a list of the topology clouds for all the domains for which the user has permission to access.
This API requires either 'Super admin' or 'Multi-Domain admin' privileges.
For users with 'Super admin' permission, the context parameter is optional. If a context is not specified, the Global context will be used.
For users with 'Multi-Domain' privilege, the context parameter is required.
To get the context parameter, call the /domains/ API and use the id included in the returned domain DTO.
Use the optional name parameter to restrict the results to topology clouds that contain the specified string.
The default pagination is start=0 and count=50.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: The starting element offset to include in the returned results

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Number of elements to include in the results

responseMessages.message: Only users with 'Super admin' permissions can access Global domain map
You do not have access permission to domain id: domainId
Wrong type provided - allowable values are: joined or non-joined

operations.notes:
Create a new topology cloud in the Interactive Map by joining existing clouds together.
This API requires either 'Super admin' or 'Multi-Domain admin' privileges.
For users with 'Super admin' permission, the context parameter is optional. If a context is not specified, the Global context will be used.
For users with 'Multi-Domain' privilege, the context parameter is required.
To get the context parameter, call the /domains/ API and use the id included in the returned domain DTO.
The body must include at least two topology cloud ids that are on a single domain.
If the context parameter is not provided, then the API will use the context id of the first member of the members list in the body.
Clouds included in the members list of the body must not be joined clouds or members of another joined cloud.
If the provided body does not specify a joined cloud name, the newly created topology cloud will be given the name of the first member of the members list in the body.

responseMessages.message: You do not have access permission to the domain for cloud with id ID
Cloud with id ID was not found in the provided MSSP domain
You must provide 2 or more clouds
Cloud id: cloudId does not belong to domain: Context
Cloud with id ID is a joined cloud
Cloud with id ID is a member of a joined cloud

operations.notes:
Returns information about all clouds in the topology.
This API includes the ID of the cloud, the number of routes that point to the cloud, and the relevant devices (including the management_id) that have routes that point to the cloud.
This information can be used to identify missing devices that may need to be added to the topology or to identify clouds that are candidates for being joined.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: The starting element offset to include in the returned results

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Number of elements to include in the results

responseMessages.message: User is not permitted to access the specified domain

operations.notes:
Returns information about a specific cloud in the topology.
This API includes the ID of the cloud, the number of routes that point to the cloud, and the relevant devices (including the management_id) that have routes that point to the cloud.
This information can be used to identify missing devices that may need to be added to the topology or to identify clouds that are candidates for being joined.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: The starting element offset to include in the returned results
